attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation knife countertop: knife is not a agent
  atLocation glass countertop: glass is not a agent
  atLocation egg countertop: egg is not a agent
  atLocation bread countertop: bread is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: put a glass and knife onto a kitchen shelf
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen counter area with several objects placed on a metallic countertop, situated below wooden upper cabinets. The primary objects relevant to the domain and the instruction are a knife and a glass (which is a type of receptacle).

1.  **Objects and their types:**
    *   **Knife:** A single knife is present. It has a silver blade and a dark, possibly black or dark brown, handle. It is classified as an `object` of type `knife`.
    *   **Glass:** A single glass is visible. It is a translucent, purplish-pink or lavender-colored container with a brown, pump-style dispenser top, resembling a soap or lotion dispenser. For the purpose of this domain, it is treated as a `receptacle` (specifically, a `glass` as per the instruction, though visually it's a dispenser bottle). It is classified as an `object` of type `receptacle`.
    *   **Bread:** A loaf of bread is on the counter. It is an `object` but is not relevant to the current instruction.
    *   **Egg:** A single egg is on the counter. It is an `object` but is not relevant to the current instruction.
    *   **Microwave:** A black microwave is partially visible on the left. It is a `receptacle` and is `openable`.
    *   **Cabinets:** The wooden upper cabinets are visible. They are `receptacle`s and are `openable`.

2.  **Quantities:**
    *   Knife: 1
    *   Glass (Dispenser): 1
    *   Bread: 1
    *   Egg: 1
    *   Microwave: 1
    *   Cabinets: 2 (or more, but at least two doors are visible)

3.  **Spatial Relationships:**
    *   The **knife** is resting on the metallic countertop.
    *   The **glass (dispenser)** is also resting on the metallic countertop, to the right of the bread and to the left of the egg.
    *   The **bread** is on the countertop, to the left of the glass.
    *   The **egg** is on the countertop, to the right of the glass.
    *   The **microwave** is positioned on the countertop to the far left.
    *   The **cabinets** are mounted on the wall above the countertop.
    *   The **knife** and **glass** are not currently held by any agent and are not inside any receptacle. They are both `atLocation` the countertop.
    *   The **microwave** is not opened.

4.  **Current State of Objects (Relevant to Planning):**
    *   **Knife:** Not held by any agent (`not (holdsAny ?a)` for the knife). Not in any receptacle. Not sliced. Not clean (as per domain, `isClean` is for objects cleaned in sink, which is not applicable here).
    *   **Glass (Dispenser):** Not held by any agent. Not in any receptacle. Not clean. Not hot. Not cool.
    *   **Microwave:** Not opened. Not toggled on.
    *   **Cabinets:** Not opened.

5.  **Visual Properties:**
    *   **Knife:** Silver blade, dark handle. Positioned horizontally on the counter.
    *   **Glass (Dispenser):** Translucent purplish-pink body, brown pump top. Positioned upright on the counter.
    *   **Bread:** Golden-brown, rectangular loaf with a textured top.
    *   **Egg:** Light beige, oval-shaped.
    *   **Microwave:** Black exterior, digital display panel visible.
    *   **Countertop:** Metallic, reflective surface.
    *   **Cabinets:** Light brown wood grain finish.
    *   The lighting in the scene creates soft shadows, indicating a light source from the upper left. The overall scene is static, with no agent present or in motion. The instruction requires the robot to pick up the glass and the knife and place them onto a kitchen shelf (which is likely one of the openable cabinets or a shelf within them).

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_glass_and_knife_on_shelf)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        knife - knife
        glass - receptacle
        countertop - receptacle
        cabinet_left - receptacle
        cabinet_right - receptacle
        microwave - receptacle
        bread - object
        egg - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot countertop))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_left))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_right))
        (not (atLocation robot microwave))

        ; Objects are on the countertop
        (atLocation robot countertop) ; Assuming robot starts near countertop for simplicity, or it can go there
        (atLocation knife countertop)
        (atLocation glass countertop)
        (atLocation bread countertop)
        (atLocation egg countertop)

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable microwave)
        (openable cabinet_left)
        (openable cabinet_right)

        ; Receptacles are not opened
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (opened cabinet_left))
        (not (opened cabinet_right))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; Objects are not in any receptacle
        (not (inReceptacle knife countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle glass countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle bread countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle egg countertop))

        ; Objects are not cleaned, heated, cooled, sliced, or toggled
        (not (isClean knife))
        (not (isClean glass))
        (not (isHot knife))
        (not (isHot glass))
        (not (isCool knife))
        (not (isCool glass))
        (not (isSliced knife))
        (not (isSliced glass))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The knife is in one of the cabinets (shelf)
            (inReceptacle knife cabinet_left)
            ; The glass is in one of the cabinets (shelf)
            (inReceptacle glass cabinet_right)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_glass_and_knife_on_shelf)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        knife - knife
        glass - receptacle
        countertop - receptacle
        cabinet_left - receptacle
        cabinet_right - receptacle
        microwave - receptacle
        bread - object
        egg - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot countertop))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_left))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_right))
        (not (atLocation robot microwave))

        ; Objects are on the countertop
        (atLocation robot countertop) ; Assuming robot starts near countertop for simplicity, or it can go there
        (atLocation knife countertop)
        (atLocation glass countertop)
        (atLocation bread countertop)
        (atLocation egg countertop)

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable microwave)
        (openable cabinet_left)
        (openable cabinet_right)

        ; Receptacles are not opened
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (opened cabinet_left))
        (not (opened cabinet_right))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; Objects are not in any receptacle
        (not (inReceptacle knife countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle glass countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle bread countertop))
        (not (inReceptacle egg countertop))

        ; Objects are not cleaned, heated, cooled, sliced, or toggled
        (not (isClean knife))
        (not (isClean glass))
        (not (isHot knife))
        (not (isHot glass))
        (not (isCool knife))
        (not (isCool glass))
        (not (isSliced knife))
        (not (isSliced glass))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            ; The knife is in one of the cabinets (shelf)
            (inReceptacle knife cabinet_left)
            ; The glass is in one of the cabinets (shelf)
            (inReceptacle glass cabinet_right)
        )
    )
)

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a kitchen countertop area with several objects placed on it, under a wooden cabinet. The primary objects of interest are a loaf of bread, a knife, and a glass dispenser (which can be interpreted as a "glass" for the task). The countertop itself is a flat, grey surface, and the wooden cabinet above has a visible grain pattern.

1. Objects and their types:
   - Knife: A single knife with a metallic blade and a dark handle is present. It is classified as an "object" and specifically as a "knife" in the domain.
   - Glass: A transparent glass dispenser with a brown pump top is present. For the purpose of the domain, this is treated as a "glass" (an object, not a receptacle, since it is not designed to hold other objects for storage in this context).
   - Bread: A loaf of bread is present, but it is not relevant to the current instruction and is not defined in the domain types for this task.
   - Microwave: A black microwave is partially visible on the left side of the countertop. It is a "receptacle" and is openable.
   - Cabinet: The wooden cabinet above the countertop is a "receptacle" and is openable. It is currently closed.

2. Quantities:
   - One knife.
   - One glass (dispenser).
   - One microwave (receptacle).
   - One cabinet (receptacle).

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The knife is lying on the countertop, to the right of the glass dispenser.
   - The glass dispenser is on the countertop, to the left of the knife and to the right of the bread.
   - The bread is on the countertop, to the left of the glass dispenser.
   - The microwave is on the countertop, to the left of the bread.
   - The cabinet is above the countertop and spans the width of the scene.
   - The robot is not currently at any object's location (initial state).

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The knife is on the countertop and is not being held by the agent.
   - The glass (dispenser) is on the countertop and is not being held by the agent.
   - The microwave is closed and not opened.
   - The cabinet is closed and not opened.
   - Neither the knife nor the glass has been cleaned, heated, cooled, sliced, or toggled.
   - The knife is not in any receptacle.
   - The glass is not in any receptacle.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - The knife has a metallic blade and a dark (possibly black) handle.
   - The glass dispenser is transparent with a brown pump top.
   - The bread is golden brown.
   - The microwave is black with a digital display.
   - The countertop is grey.
   - The cabinet is light brown with a wood grain pattern.

The instruction is to put a glass and knife onto a kitchen shelf. The kitchen shelf is interpreted as the cabinet above the countertop, which is a receptacle. The robot must first go to the countertop to pick up the glass and knife, then go to the cabinet, open it, and put the objects inside.

Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.

attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_glass_and_knife_on_shelf)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        knife1 - knife
        glass1 - object
        microwave1 - microwave
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        countertop1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 countertop1)
        (inReceptacle knife1 countertop1)
        (inReceptacle glass1 countertop1)
        (openable microwave1)
        (openable cabinet1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (opened cabinet1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean knife1))
        (not (isClean glass1))
        (not (isHot knife1))
        (not (isHot glass1))
        (not (isCool knife1))
        (not (isCool glass1))
        (not (isSliced knife1))
        (not (isSliced glass1))
        (not (isOn knife1))
        (not (isOn glass1))
        (not (isToggled knife1))
        (not (isToggled glass1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle knife1 cabinet1)
        (inReceptacle glass1 cabinet1)
        (opened cabinet1)
    ))
)
```

You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
